label: Viral conjunctivitis without contact lens or vision red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit uncomplicated viral conjunctivitis.
found: Your clinician diagnosed viral conjunctivitis. We did not find contact lens risk, vision change, eye pain, trauma, or cornea emergency signs today.
home: Avoid touching or rubbing the eyes.
Do not share towels, eye makeup, or contact lens supplies.
meds: Use eye medicines or allergy medicines only if prescribed or recommended.
Do not use leftover eye drops unless your clinician said they are safe.
return: Eye pain, light sensitivity, vision change, severe redness, or worsening swelling.
Contact lens use with worsening symptoms.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, eye clinic, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated viral conjunctivitis.
No contact lens use, eye pain, photophobia, vision change, corneal concern, trauma, or specialist-directed ophthalmology plan.
exclusion: Contact lens use, eye pain, photophobia, vision change, corneal ulcer/keratitis concern, eye trauma, chemical exposure, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed ophthalmology plan.
source_audit_notes: cdc.conjunctivitis, medlineplus.conjunctivitis supports patient-education framing for viral conjunctivitis and boundary design. Autonomous expansion approved this narrow v1 only when clinician diagnosis is documented and red flags requiring a separate pathway are absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated viral conjunctivitis
red flags requiring a separate pathway absent and documented

label: Resolved anterior nosebleed without packing or anticoagulation red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit uncomplicated resolved anterior nosebleed.
found: Your clinician diagnosed resolved anterior nosebleed. We did not find red flags requiring a separate emergency pathway today.
home: Avoid picking, probing, or putting objects into the affected area.
Use the care plan your clinician recommended.
Keep follow-up if your clinician asked for recheck.
meds: Use medicines only if prescribed or recommended.
Do not add leftover antibiotics, drops, sprays, or packing care unless your clinician told you to.
return: Bleeding that will not stop, worsening pain, fever, swelling, or feeling very ill.

inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated resolved anterior nosebleed.
No emergency red flags, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Red flags requiring a separate ED pathway, unstable vital signs, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

label: Triceps tendinitis without rupture or infection red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit uncomplicated triceps tendinitis.
found: Your clinician diagnosed triceps tendinitis. We did not find tendon rupture, infection, neurovascular compromise, or another emergency pathway today.
home: Rest the sore area as instructed.
Use ice, gentle motion, activity limits, or gradual return to activity only as your clinician recommended.
Avoid activity that causes sharp or worsening pain until you are improving.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not combine medicines unless your clinician said it is safe.
return: Worsening severe pain, new swelling, numbness, weakness, color change, or trouble using the arm.
A pop with loss of strength, new deformity, or concern for tendon rupture.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, drainage, or symptoms not improving as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated triceps tendinitis.
No tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, infection, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, severe pain, neurovascular compromise, infection concern, inability to use the limb, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports tendinitis framing, activity modification, symptom care, and warning signs. Autonomous expansion approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed triceps tendinitis without rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, infection, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated triceps tendinitis
no tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, infection, or specialist-directed plan documented
